title: Fix Candid opt variant encoding for role submission

requirements.summary: Convert plain string role values to correct Candid opt variant format ([{ customer: null }], [{ driver: null }], [{ admin: null }]) in all frontend role submission calls
acceptanceCriteria: Submitting role 'customer' sends [{ customer: null }] to the backend, not the string "customer"
Submitting role 'driver' sends [{ driver: null }] to the backend
Submitting role 'admin' sends [{ admin: null }] to the backend
The role is wrapped in an array to represent the Candid opt type (e.g., [{ customer: null }])
No changes are made to auth logic, route guards, admin logic, booking, pricing, GPS, or UI design
Role selection from SelectRolePage and RoleSelectionWarningModal both use the corrected encoding
The backend no longer returns a Candid variant encoding error when a role is saved

file_operations.description: Add a helper function convertRoleToVariant that maps plain role strings ('customer', 'driver', 'admin') to the Candid opt variant format ([{ customer: null }], [{ driver: null }], [{ admin: null }]). Apply this conversion in the updateRole mutation before calling actor.setMyRole, ensuring the role parameter is correctly wrapped as a Candid opt variant.

Update the role selection handler to use the convertRoleToVariant helper from useQueries.ts before calling the updateRole mutation, ensuring that the selected role ('customer' or 'driver') is converted to the correct Candid opt variant format before submission.

Update the role selection handler in the modal to use the convertRoleToVariant helper from useQueries.ts before calling the updateRole mutation, ensuring that the selected role ('customer' or 'driver') is converted to the correct Candid opt variant format before submission.
